title: SIMD & Vectorized CPU Code for Graphics
description: Covers AVX2 intrinsics for vectorized matrix multiplication, frustum culling, and mesh processing, explaining the data layout requirements (SOA format, 256-bit alignment) that enable full SIMD lane utilization. CPU-side SIMD is critical for high-frequency operations like frustum culling 10,000+ objects per frame and the GP Engine's math library uses AVX2 throughout. Students will implement an AVX2-vectorized frustum cull that tests 8 AABBs per CPU instruction, measure the scalar vs. SIMD throughput improvement, and explain why SOA layout is mandatory for SIMD efficiency.
